title: tithymalopsis olivacea
intro: Tithymalopsis olivacea, also known as the olive calotropis, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family that is native to various parts of the world. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Tithymalopsis olivacea include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through the use of its leaves, which can be made into teas or infusions, or through the extraction of its roots and stems to create tinctures and ointments.
